question.text: A complex with Δo > P (Pairing energy) will be ____ spin.
question.explanation: Electrons prefer to pair up in lower orbitals.

question.text: Magnetic field at axial point of short magnet B_axial = ____.
The resonant frequency f₀ of an LCR circuit is f₀ = ____.
Methemoglobinemia (Blue Baby Syndrome) is caused by excess of ____ in drinking water.
question.explanation: Interferes with oxygen transport in babies.

question.text: Question 10 (Easy) on Evolution: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 10 regarding Evolution.

question.text: Apparent depth of an object submerged in liquid of refractive index n is h_app = ____.
question.explanation: Bending towards normal makes it look shallower.

question.text: 1 micrometer is equal to:
Equation v^2 - u^2 = 2as relates ____ and displacement.
Fluorine forms only one oxoacid which is ____.
Water-gas shift reaction use ____ catalyst.
Direct nitration of Aniline gives signficant amount of meta product. Why?
question.option: In acid, aniline forms anilinium ion (meta directing)

question.text: Question 29 (Easy) on Environmental Issues: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 29 regarding Environmental Issues.

question.text: Which ion is specifically needed for neurotransmitter release from vesicles?
question.explanation: Calcium influx at synaptic knob triggers exocytosis.

question.text: Movement of water always occurs from ____ DPD to ____ DPD.
question.explanation: From low demand to high demand.

question.text: Reductive amination of carbonyls mentioned.
Magnetism and Matter standardization complete.
Wurtz reaction cannot prepare methane. True? ____.
question.explanation: Coupling process needs two alkyl radicals.

question.text: Question 109 (Hard) on Human Reproduction: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 109 regarding Human Reproduction.

question.text: Question 77 (Medium) on Organisms and Populations: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 77 regarding Organisms and Populations.

question.text: Photosynthetic potential refers to ____.
question.explanation: Determined by genetics and enzyme kinetics.

question.text: Question 104 (Hard) on Environmental Issues: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 104 regarding Environmental Issues.

question.text: Radius ratio (r+/r-) for tetrahedral void is ____.
Pollination is a mechanism of ____.
question.explanation: Movement of pollen from anther to stigma.

question.text: Moment of Inertia of a uniform disc (M, R) about center axis is ____.
question.explanation: Mass is distributed from 0 to R, integration gives factor of 1/2.

question.text: Process of nitrate reduction was first done in ____.
Additional practice question 107 on Anatomy of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 107 in the Anatomy of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Question 18 (Easy) on Principles of Inheritance and Variation: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 18 regarding Principles of Inheritance and Variation.

question.text: Final check for Living World: 120 count reached?
High Auxin/Cytokinin ratio favors ____ formation in tissue culture.
Marasmus is deficiency of ____.
The word 'Organic' was first used by Berzelius based on ____ force theory.
question.explanation: Old belief that organic compounds can't be lab-made.

question.text: Respiratory pathway involves both breakdown and synthesis, hence it's an ____ pathway.
question.explanation: Intermediate metabolites are used for other biosyntheses.

question.text: Study of algae is called ____.
Magnetic field inside the core of a toroid is B = ____.
Pyrimidines found in nucleic acids are ____.
Succus entericus is the name for ____.
question.explanation: Secretion of Crypts and goblet cells.

question.text: Coulomb's Law relates force between two ____ charges.
Question 29 (Easy) on Biotechnology: Principles and Processes: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 29 regarding Biotechnology: Principles and Processes.

question.text: Talc is a ____ silicate.
Structural isomerism arising from ambidentate ligands is ____.
Radius ratio (r+/r-) for octahedral void is ____.
question.explanation: Ratio of sphere sizes for stability.

question.text: Soil provides minerals primarily through ____ of rocks.
Comparison: C-H bond energy in CH4 vs Si-H in SiH4? ____ is stronger.
question.explanation: Smaller overlap in SiH4 leads to lower stability.

question.text: Work to launch m from surface to infinity? ____.
Pressure in Efferent arteriole is ____ than Afferent arteriole.
question.explanation: Wait, narrower lumen increases resistance, so pressure in glomerulus is high.

question.text: Carnot theorem states that no engine is ____ than Carnot between same T's.
Enterogastric reflex is triggered by ____.
Nervous system of frog involves ____ pairs of cranial nerves.
A spring balance measures ____.
question.explanation: Measures the tension caused by gravity.

question.text: Question 95 (Hard) on Ecosystem: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 95 regarding Ecosystem.

question.text: The Solid State standardization complete (120 questions)?
Question 107 (Hard) on Organisms and Populations: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 107 regarding Organisms and Populations.

question.text: Identification requires intensive ____ studies.
Fundamental freq of open pipe of length L is f. For closed pipe of same L, it is ____.
Question 91 (Hard) on Biodiversity and Conservation: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 91 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Light waves from a distant source (like Sun) reach Earth as ____ wavefronts.
Pepsinogen is converted to active Pepsin by ____.
Human stomach pH is approx ____ for digestion.
ATP synthesis in chloroplast is explained by ____ hypothesis.
question.explanation: Relies on proton gradient across membrane.

question.text: Large number of male gametes are produced to compensate for ____.
Question 84 (Hard) on Human Reproduction: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 84 regarding Human Reproduction.

question.text: Density has dimensions:
Question 79 (Medium) on Organisms and Populations: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 79 regarding Organisms and Populations.

question.text: Question 13 (Easy) on Human Reproduction: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 13 regarding Human Reproduction.

question.text: A full-wave rectifier requires at least ____ diodes.
question.explanation: To rectify both halves of AC cycle.

question.text: Optical isomerism is shown by molecules with no element of ____.
question.explanation: Chiral molecules rotate plane polarized light.

question.text: Nitrobacter converts nitrite to ____.
Final Q: Verified 120 count for Gravitation module.
Question 72 (Medium) on Human Health and Disease: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 72 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: Density of LDP is ____ than HDP.
question.explanation: Due to branching preventing close packing.

question.text: Work function of Cesium is approx ____ eV.
question.explanation: One of the lowest work functions.

question.text: The slope of Ellingham diagram lines for most metal oxides is ____.
question.explanation: Because entropy decreases when gas is consumed.

question.text: Gap between two neurons is ____.
question.explanation: Comprises pre-synaptic, cleft and post-synaptic membranes.

question.text: If base region is made thicker, β will ____.
question.explanation: Transistor gain drops if carriers don't reach collector.

question.text: The relative lowering of vapor pressure is equal to the ____.
Chemicals produced by endocrine glands that act as inter-cellular messengers? ____.
question.explanation: Produced in trace amounts and are non-nutrient chemicals.

question.text: Transformer cannot work on battery because battery provides ____.
Question 44 (Medium) on Human Reproduction: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 44 regarding Human Reproduction.

question.text: Electric potential energy of a dipole in uniform field is U = ____.
Question 64 (Medium) on Ecosystem: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 64 regarding Ecosystem.

question.text: K2Cr2O7 is preferred over Na2Cr2O7 as a primary standard because it is ____.
FBD of a falling stone ignoring air contains only ____.
Triple point of copper? Non-existent at low P. Focus on chapter... Thermal expansion occurs because potential energy well is ____.
White fumes of AlCl3 in moist air are due to ____.
Relation between displacement x and velocity v is an ____ curve.
Question 24 (Easy) on Biotechnology: Principles and Processes: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 24 regarding Biotechnology: Principles and Processes.

question.text: Universal recipient blood group is ____.
question.explanation: Lacks antibodies Anti-A and Anti-B in plasma.

question.text: Neutral ligands are named as the ____.
question.option: Molecules name (with exceptions like Ammine, Aqua)

question.text: Boiling points: 1° > 2° > 3° amines (isomeric). Correct? ____.
question.explanation: Due to decreasing extent of intermolecular H-bonding.

question.text: Average rate depends on the ____ of time interval.
Question 50 (Medium) on Microbes in Human Welfare: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 50 regarding Microbes in Human Welfare.

question.text: Upcoming chapter is Alternating Current.
Gymnosperms do not show annual rings? No, they do in ____.
Question 105 (Hard) on Evolution: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 105 regarding Evolution.

question.text: Stamens united into two bundles (Pea) are ____.
Which of the following is a non-polar molecule?
Entanglement means particles are ____ regardless of distance.
Cu(I) ion is unstable in aqueous solution and undergoes ____.
Medium 40 complete?
Question 1 (Easy) on Biodiversity and Conservation: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 1 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Values of Boltzmann constant is approx ____ J/K.
Phase difference of 2π corresponds to path difference of ____.
Total number of questions in this standard file? ____.
Kroll's process is used for the extraction of ____.
question.explanation: Uses Magnesium to reduce refined chlorides.

question.text: Power delivered at const tau and w. P = ____.
Question 93 (Hard) on Organisms and Populations: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 93 regarding Organisms and Populations.

question.text: AgCl dissolves in NH4OH due to formation of ____ complex.
Isotope of Carbon used for dating organic samples is ____.
Hardness of water is due to presence of soluble salts of ____ and ____.
Physisorption involves weak ____ forces.
Correct order of reactivity with water: ____.
Question 100 (Hard) on Ecosystem: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 100 regarding Ecosystem.

question.text: Degrees of freedom for monatomic gas? ____.
Penicillium belongs to ____ group of fungi.
Lehemoglobin is produced by ____.
question.option: Partnership (plant DNA for heme? No. Plant Globin, Rhizobium Heme)

question.text: Question 119 (Hard) on Reproductive Health: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 119 regarding Reproductive Health.

question.text: Standard for atomic weights is isotope ____.
Centromere is also called ____ constriction.
Which series of spectral lines in H-atom is partly in visible and partly in UV?
question.option: Balmer series? No, Balmer is mainly visible/near UV. Correct is Balmer.
question.explanation: Series starts from 656nm and ends at 364nm.

question.text: Question 115 (Hard) on Molecular Basis of Inheritance: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 115 regarding Molecular Basis of Inheritance.

question.text: An alternating current is one which ____ with time.
Which of the following is a sink for CO?
Question 27 (Easy) on Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 27 regarding Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.

question.text: Change in length per unit original length is ____ strain.
Lipids are generally ____ in water.
Question 60 (Medium) on Biotechnology: Principles and Processes: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 60 regarding Biotechnology: Principles and Processes.

question.text: Question 114 (Hard) on Organisms and Populations: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 114 regarding Organisms and Populations.

question.text: AgI is ____ in ammonia.
Bond angle in Water (H2O) is approx ____ degrees.
question.explanation: Bent shape due to lone pair repulsion.

question.text: Diffraction is prominent when the obstacle size is ____ to the wavelength.
Commercial product Agar is obtained from ____ and ____.
question.explanation: Used to grow microbes and in jellies.

question.text: Question 83 (Hard) on Biodiversity and Conservation: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 83 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Metabolites like alkaloids, rubber, spices, scents, gums are ____.
question.explanation: Non-essential for immediate survival but have industrial/ecological roles.

question.text: Sucrose is converted to glucose and fructose by enzyme ____.
Question 48 (Medium) on Microbes in Human Welfare: Application of concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 48 regarding Microbes in Human Welfare.

question.text: Reduction potential of Zn is -0.76 V. If [Zn 2+] decreases, the potential becomes ____ negative.
question.explanation: Based on Nernst equation: E = E° - (0.059/2) log(1/[Zn2+]).

question.text: Tension in string of 10kg mass in lift moving DOWN with a=2 m/s^2? ____ N.
The work function for a metal surface is 4 eV. The threshold wavelength is ____ nm.
Main conducting elements of phloem in angiosperms are ____.
Question 104 (Hard) on Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants: Advanced analysis and synthesis.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 104 regarding Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants.

question.text: Thermal decomposition of higher alkanes to lower ones is ____.
All questions follow 6-line schema.
Botanical Gardens have specimens labeled with ____.
Question 8 (Easy) on Human Health and Disease: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 8 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: There are ____ pairs of ribs in humans.
question.explanation: Each rib is a thin flat bone.

question.text: Question 9 (Easy) on Biotechnology and its Applications: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 9 regarding Biotechnology and its Applications.

question.text: Why C4 plants thrive in low CO2?
question.option: PEPcase has very high affinity for CO2

question.text: In the above series cells, moles of Cu deposited?
Direction of movement in Xylem is always ____.
Question 13 (Easy) on Evolution: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 13 regarding Evolution.

question.text: Atmetaphase I, bivalents arrange on ____ plate.
question.explanation: Random orientation leads to independent assortment.

question.text: Unit of spring constant k is ____.
Additional practice question 101 on Anatomy of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 101 in the Anatomy of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Antibiotics are substances produced by microorganisms to ____ other microorganisms.
Cell A (psi_s = -10, psi_p = 5), Cell B (psi_s = -15, psi_p = 5). Water moves:
Refrigerator transfers heat from cold reservoir to hot reservoir using ____.
Reaction: Zn + NaOH (heat) -> ____ + H2.
Dimensional formula for Moment of Inertia is ____.
Yeast reproduces by ____.
Question 28 (Easy) on Human Health and Disease: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 28 regarding Human Health and Disease.

question.text: Ideal heat engine cycle is ____ cycle.
question.explanation: Most efficient cycle between two temps.

question.text: Additional practice question 117 on Anatomy of Flowering Plants: Advanced level concepts.
question.explanation: Detailed explanation for additional question 117 in the Anatomy of Flowering Plants module.

question.text: Hydrogen shows dual behavior, resembling both ____ and ____.
question.explanation: Electronic configuration ns1 (like alkali) and needs 1e- for octet (like halogens).

question.text: The cross-bridge is formed between ____ head and ____ active site.
Calculation of i for K2SO4 (complete dissociation): i = ____.
Cell wall of algae is made of cellulose, galactans, mannans and ____.
question.explanation: Distinct from higher plant cell walls.

question.text: Which element is present in iron-sulphur centers of ETC?
A pump of 3kW raises water to 15m in 1 min. Volume of water? ____ L.
Roundworms belong to Phylum ____.
The study of production of electricity from energy released during spontaneous chemical reactions is called ____.
Resonance column experiment: Diameter of tube = d. End correction e = ____.
question.explanation: Approximate shift of antinode beyond open end.

question.text: Decrease in radius calculation for 1m wire extended 1mm uses ____.
Stereochemical principles of SN1/SN2 included.
Protein involved in blood clotting? ____.
Question 18 (Easy) on Evolution: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 18 regarding Evolution.

question.text: Radius of a nucleus R is proportional to ____.
question.explanation: R = R₀ A^(1/3) where R₀ ≈ 1.2 x 10^-15 m.

question.text: With increase in temperature, Maxwell-Boltzmann peak shifts to ____ and becomes ____.
If rate doubles when temp rises from 300K to 310K, Ea is approx ____.

question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 112 regarding Biotechnology: Principles and Processes.

question.text: Flow of electrons from PSII to PSI and then to NADP+ is called ____ scheme.
TSH stimulates synthesis and secretion of thyroid hormones from ____ gland.
Question 30 (Easy) on Biotechnology: Principles and Processes: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 30 regarding Biotechnology: Principles and Processes.

question.text: Red Tides in oceans are caused by rapid multiplication of ____.
question.explanation: Toxins released can kill marine life.

question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 68 regarding Microbes in Human Welfare.

question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 27 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Who first saw and described a live cell?

question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 2 regarding Organisms and Populations.

question.text: Physics batch 1 progress: 2/4 chapters.
Metallic character decreases as electronegativity ____.
Final check for Coordination: 120 count reached?
Organelle known as 'Kitchen of the cell' in plants is ____.
Work done by air in inflating balloon at constant P_atm is ____.
Question 19 (Easy) on Microbes in Human Welfare: Basic definition and concepts.
question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 19 regarding Microbes in Human Welfare.

question.text: Vic-dihalides have two halogens on ____ carbon atoms.
Normality of same 18 M H2SO4 is ____ N.
Bread mould is scientificly known as ____.

question.explanation: Educational explanation for question 75 regarding Biodiversity and Conservation.

question.text: Error in sum/difference is equal to ____ of absolute errors.
Auxins initiate ____ in stem cuttings.
